name: Anise Biscotti
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325 degrees F.
2. Combine flour, anise seeds, baking powder, baking soda, and salt in a bowl.
3. Whisk together eggs, egg whites, lemon zest, and lemon juice in another bowl, then add the dry ingredients; mix well.
4. Shape the dough into two logs on a floured surface, each about 14 inches long and 1 1/2 inches thick.
5. Place the logs on a prepared baking sheet and bake for 20 to 25 minutes, or until firm to the touch.
6. Remove from oven and let cool completely before proceeding.
7. Reduce oven temperature to 300 degrees F.
8. Cut the logs diagonally into 1/2-inch thick slices.
9. Stand the slices upright on the baking sheet and bake for an additional 40 minutes, or until crisp.
10. Allow to cool completely before storing.
Makes about 4 dozen biscotti.
39 calories per biscotti: 1 g protein, 0 g fat, 8 g carbohydrate; 31 mg sodium; 9 mg cholesterol.
Source: Eating Well November/December 1992. Enjoy!
